LAW AND MOTION 301, RE DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT TESLA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY IS GRANTED. TESLA'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. A PROPOSED ORDER HAS BEEN SUBMITTED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TESLA, INC.'S, A TEXAS CORPORATION, MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Tesla, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. Tesla's request for judicial notice is granted.

See Word minutes for complete ruling.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
